title: Canonical queue state lags additive continuity execution records
summary: The canonical queue still marks M-001 completed and contains no active continuity-owned successor, while additive continuity records and an M-002 proposal document subsequent decision-lineage and integrity work. These are distinct state layers: the queue is the canonical coordination projection, while committed records preserve work actually performed. The later records do not retroactively make M-002 canonical and the stale projection does not erase completed continuity work.

status_relations.observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains completed M-001 and no active or queued continuity-owned successor; later commits contain an M-002 proposal and continuity integrity records.
status_relations.inferred: Consumers that read only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can undercount continuity work and incorrectly conclude the team has produced nothing after M-001.
status_relations.proposed: Adopt M-002 into the canonical queue or add a deterministic projection process that reconciles accepted additive queue updates into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json without overwriting other teams.
status_relations.superseded: Any interpretation that additive queue-update artifacts automatically change canonical ownership or status is superseded by the repository's explicit separation between canonical queue state and additive proposals.
status_relations.unresolved: No accepted reconciliation record or canonical M-002 entry was observable at this run, and no branch-protection evidence proves that all future projection changes pass the continuity integrity gate.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json and the commits referenced above. Reject this record if ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains an accepted active M-002 entry at the observed revision, if the cited commits do not contain the described additive proposal and integrity record, or if repository policy explicitly defines queue-update artifacts as canonical state mutations.
